extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 19km remaining on Civiglio, MAAS couldn't distance him in finale, POGAČAR outsprinted at 150m to become only rider to win first two Lombardia appearances
tactical_patterns: POGAČAR attacked 19km out on Civiglio rather than waiting for final climb, forcing MAAS to chase entire finale
UAE's HARPER did crucial late domestique work enabling POGAČAR's early move while JUMBO lacked similar depth
LANDA stayed 10-15 seconds back rather than bridging, calculating podium safer than contesting sprint with POGAČAR/MAAS

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Won sprint despite MAAS launching at 150m, suggesting climbing attacks don't compromise his finish speed
rider_signals.MAAS Remco: positive: Multiple attacks couldn't drop POGAČAR but defended second place, 'changed rider in last few weeks' signals late-season peak timing
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: positive: First Monument podium at age 33, tactical maturity to sit at 10-15s gap rather than overcommit
rider_signals.HARPER Chris: positive: Departing domestique delivered 'man of the match' pacing on final climbs, UAE depth underestimated
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: neutral: Sixth in final race maintains decade-long top-10 Monument consistency despite retirement timing
